     7→  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
     8→  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
     9→  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs → "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
    10→  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    11→    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    12→    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")

    18→  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
    19→  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
    20→  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
    21→  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
    22→  def restore_from_state(user:)
    23→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    24→
    25→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    26→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"

    34→    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    35→    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    36→    container = create_sidecar(

   112→  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
   113→  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
   114→  def check_login(user:)
   115→    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
   116→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   117→
   118→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   119→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   120→    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running
   121→
   122→    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
   123→    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
   124→    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
   125→      subnet = subnet_for(user)
   126→      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
   127→      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
   128→      container.exec([
   129→        "sh", "-c",
   130→        "tailscale up --reset" \
   131→        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
   132→        "#{tag_flag}" \
   133→        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
   134→        " --timeout=120s &"
   135→      ])
   136→      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
   137→      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   138→    end
   139→
   140→    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
   141→    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])

   143→      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)

   145→      when "Running"
   146→        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
   147→        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)

   153→          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
   154→          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   155→        }

   175→  def disable(user:)
   176→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?
   177→
   178→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   179→
   180→    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
   181→    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   182→      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    cleanup_sidecar(user)
   186→
   187→    user.update!(
   188→      tailscale_state: "disabled",
   189→      tailscale_container_id: nil,
   190→      tailscale_network: nil
   191→    )
   192→    delete_auth_key(user)
   193→    user
   194→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   195→    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   196→  end
   197→
   198→  def status(user:)
   199→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
   200→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   201→
   202→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   203→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   204→
   205→    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
   206→    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
   207→      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
   208→      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
   209→    end
   210→
   211→    result = {
   212→      running: running,
   213→      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
   214→      network: user.tailscale_network,
   215→      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
   216→      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    if running
   220→      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   221→      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   222→
   223→      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   224→      if status_out.first.any?
   225→        begin
   226→          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   227→          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
   228→          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   229→          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
   230→          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
   231→          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
   232→        rescue JSON::ParserError
   233→          # Status not available yet
   234→        end
   235→      end
   236→    else
   237→      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
   238→      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
   239→      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
   240→      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
   241→      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
   242→    end
   243→
   244→    result
   245→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   246→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
   247→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   248→    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
   249→  end
   250→
   251→  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   252→    user = sandbox.user
   253→    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
   254→    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   255→
   256→    # Verify container exists and check if already connected
   257→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   258→    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
   259→    return sandbox if connected_networks.key?(user.tailscale_network)
   260→
   261→    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   262→
   263→    # Sysbox containers have heavier init (user namespace, rootless daemon) so
   264→    # the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after start.
   265→    retries = 0
   266→    begin
   267→      network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   268→    rescue Docker::Error::ServerError => e
   269→      raise unless e.message.include?("network sandbox") && retries < 3
   270→      retries += 1
   271→      sleep retries
   272→      retry
   273→    end
   274→
   275→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
   276→    sandbox
   277→  rescue Error
   278→    raise
   279→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   280→    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   281→  end
   282→
   283→  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   284→    user = sandbox.user
   285→    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   286→
   287→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   288→      begin
   289→        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   290→        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   291→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
   292→        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
   293→      end
   294→    end
   295→
   296→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
   297→    sandbox
   298→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   299→    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   300→  end
   301→
   302→  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
   303→    user = sandbox.user
   304→    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   305→
   306→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   307→    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
   308→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   309→    nil
   310→  end
   311→
   312→  def auth_key_path(user)
   313→    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
   314→  end
   315→
   316→  private
   317→
   318→  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
   319→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   320→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
   321→    File.write(path, auth_key)
   322→    File.chmod(0o600, path)
   323→  end
   324→
   325→  def delete_auth_key(user)
   326→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   327→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   328→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   329→    # already gone
   330→  end
   331→
   332→  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   333→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   334→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   335→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
   336→
   337→    pull_image
   338→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
   339→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
   340→    clear_tailscale_state(user)
   341→    container = create_sidecar(
   342→      name: container_name,
   343→      user: user,
   344→      network: network_name,
   345→      subnet: subnet,
   346→      auth_key: auth_key,
   347→      hostname: hostname
   348→    )
   349→    container.start
   350→
   351→    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
   352→    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)
   353→
   354→    [ network_name, container ]
   355→  end
   356→
   357→  def remove_existing_container(name)
   358→    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
   359→    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   360→    container.delete(force: true)
   361→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   362→    # No existing container
   363→  end
   364→
   365→  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
   366→  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
   367→  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
   368→  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
   369→    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
   370→    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
   371→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   372→    # already gone
   373→  end
   374→
   375→  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
   376→    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
   377→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   378→    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   379→      begin
   380→        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
   381→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   382→        container.delete(force: true)
   383→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   384→        # Already gone
   385→      end
   386→    end
   387→
   388→    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
   389→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   390→    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   391→      begin
   392→        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
   393→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   394→        # Already gone
   395→      end
   396→    end
   397→  end
   398→
   399→  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
   400→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   401→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   402→    begin
   403→      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
   404→    rescue StandardError
   405→      nil
   406→    end
   407→    begin
   408→      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
   409→    rescue StandardError
   410→      nil
   411→    end
   412→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   413→  end
   414→
   415→  def pull_image
   416→    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   417→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   418→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   419→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   420→    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
   421→  end
   422→
   423→  def subnet_for(user)
   424→    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
   425→    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?
   426→
   427→    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
   428→    begin
   429→      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
   430→      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   431→      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   432→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError

   434→    end
   435→
   436→    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
   437→    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]

   461→    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME
   462→
   463→    config = {

   466→      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,

   472→        ],

   483→        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
   484→        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
   485→        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
   486→        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
   487→      ]
   488→    else
   489→      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
   490→      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
   491→      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
   492→      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
   493→      config["Cmd"] = []
   494→    end

   496→    Docker::Container.create(config)
   497→  end
   498→end

1→class NetworkManager
     2→  SHARED_NETWORK = "sandcastle-web"
     3→  SUBNET_LOCK_KEY = 738_263_541 # arbitrary fixed key for pg_advisory_xact_lock
     4→
     5→  class Error < StandardError; end
     6→
     7→  # Ensure the per-user bridge network exists.
     8→  # Creates it if missing and persists network_name + network_subnet on the user.
     9→  # Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.
    10→  # Uses an advisory lock to serialize subnet allocation across processes.
    11→  def ensure_user_network(user)
    12→    network_name = user.network_name.presence || "sc-net-#{user.name}"
    13→
    14→    # If the stored network exists in Docker and belongs to this user, nothing to do
    15→    if user.network_name.present? && network_owned_by?(user.network_name, user.name)
    16→      return user
    17→    end
    18→
    19→    # Global advisory lock serializes subnet allocation across all users/processes
    20→    # to prevent two concurrent calls from picking the same /24.
    21→    User.transaction do
    22→      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(#{SUBNET_LOCK_KEY})")
    23→      user.lock!
    24→      # Re-check after acquiring lock (another process may have created it)
    25→      user.reload
    26→      if user.network_name.present? && network_owned_by?(user.network_name, user.name)
    27→        return user
    28→      end
    29→
    30→      subnet = network_subnet_for(user)
    31→      network_name = user.network_name.presence || "sc-net-#{user.name}"
    32→      create_network(network_name, subnet, user)
    33→      user.update!(network_name: network_name, network_subnet: subnet)
    34→    end
    35→    user
    36→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    37→    raise Error, "Failed to ensure user network for #{user.name}: #{e.message}"
    38→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique => e
    39→    raise Error, "Failed to ensure user network for #{user.name}: #{e.message}"
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  # Connect a sandbox container to its owner's per-user network.
    43→  # Calls ensure_user_network first to create the network if needed.
    44→  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    45→    user = sandbox.user
    46→    ensure_user_network(user)
    47→    user.reload
    48→
    49→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present? && user.network_name.present?
    50→
    51→    # Check if already connected before attempting (avoids swallowing real errors)
    52→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    53→    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    54→    return if connected_networks.key?(user.network_name)
    55→
    56→    network = Docker::Network.get(user.network_name)
    57→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    58→  rescue Error
    59→    raise
    60→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    61→    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox #{sandbox.full_name} to user network: #{e.message}"
    62→  end
    63→
    64→  # Disconnect a sandbox container from its owner's per-user network.
    65→  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    66→    user = sandbox.user
    67→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present? && user.network_name.present?
    68→
    69→    begin
    70→      network = Docker::Network.get(user.network_name)
    71→      network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
    72→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
    73→      # Network or container already gone — desired state reached
    74→    end
    75→  end
    76→
    77→  # Remove the user's per-user network when they have no active sandboxes.
    78→  # Active = not destroyed or archived.
    79→  def cleanup_user_network(user)
    80→    return if user.sandboxes.active.exists?
    81→    return if user.network_name.blank?
    82→
    83→    begin
    84→      network = Docker::Network.get(user.network_name)
    85→      owner = network.info.dig("Labels", "sandcastle.owner")
    86→      unless owner == user.name
    87→        Rails.logger.warn("NetworkManager: refusing to delete network #{user.network_name} — owner label mismatch (#{owner.inspect})")
    88→        return
    89→      end
    90→      network.delete
    91→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    92→      # Already gone
    93→    end
    94→
    95→    user.update!(network_name: nil, network_subnet: nil)
    96→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    97→    raise Error, "Failed to cleanup user network for #{user.name}: #{e.message}"
    98→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique => e
    99→    raise Error, "Failed to cleanup user network for #{user.name}: #{e.message}"
   100→  end
   101→
   102→  # Ensure a running sandbox container is connected to its user's per-user network.
   103→  # Used by ContainerSyncJob to reconcile network membership.
   104→  def ensure_sandbox_connected(sandbox:)
   105→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   106→
   107→    user = sandbox.user
   108→    ensure_user_network(user)
   109→    user.reload
   110→
   111→    return unless user.network_name.present?
   112→
   113→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   114→    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
   115→
   116→    unless connected_networks.key?(user.network_name)
   117→      begin
   118→        Docker::Network.get(user.network_name).connect(sandbox.container_id)
   119→        Rails.logger.info("NetworkManager: reconnected #{sandbox.full_name} to #{user.network_name}")
   120→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   121→        Rails.logger.warn("NetworkManager: failed to reconnect #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
   122→      end
   123→    end
   124→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   125→    # Container gone — ContainerSyncJob will handle this
   126→  rescue Error => e
   127→    Rails.logger.warn("NetworkManager: ensure_sandbox_connected failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
   128→  end
   129→
   130→  private
   131→
   132→  def network_exists?(name)
   133→    Docker::Network.get(name)
   134→    true
   135→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   136→    false
   137→  end
   138→
   139→  def network_owned_by?(name, expected_owner)
   140→    network = Docker::Network.get(name)
   141→    network.info.dig("Labels", "sandcastle.owner") == expected_owner
   142→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   143→    false
   144→  end
   145→
   146→  def network_subnet_for(user)
   147→    # 1. Use the subnet stored in DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
   148→    return user.network_subnet if user.network_subnet.present?
   149→
   150→    # 2. If the network already exists in Docker, read its subnet
   151→    begin
   152→      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-net-#{user.name}")
   153→      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   154→      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   155→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   156→      # fall through to generate a new subnet
   157→    end
   158→
   159→    # 3. Generate a random /24 from DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE that doesn't conflict
   160→    generate_subnet
   161→  end
   162→
   163→  def generate_subnet
   164→    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
   165→    parts = if base
   166→      base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
   167→    else
   168→      [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
   169→    end
   170→
   171→    used = existing_network_subnets
   172→
   173→    100.times do
   174→      parts[2] = rand(1..254)
   175→      candidate = "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   176→      return candidate unless used.include?(candidate)
   177→    end
   178→
   179→    # Fallback: return last candidate (Docker will reject on collision)
   180→    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   181→  end
   182→
   183→  def existing_network_subnets
   184→    Docker::Network.all.flat_map do |network|
   185→      (network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config") || []).filter_map { |c| c["Subnet"] }
   186→    end
   187→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   188→    []
   189→  end
   190→
   191→  def create_network(name, subnet, user)
   192→    existing = Docker::Network.get(name)
   193→    # Verify the existing network belongs to this user (missing label = unsafe)
   194→    owner = existing.info.dig("Labels", "sandcastle.owner")
   195→    unless owner == user.name
   196→      raise Error, "Network #{name} has no owner label or belongs to another user (#{owner.inspect}), cannot reuse"
   197→    end
   198→    existing
   199→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   200→    Docker::Network.create(
   201→      name,
   202→      "Driver" => "bridge",
   203→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.owner" => user.name },
   204→      "IPAM" => {
   205→        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
   206→      }
   207→    )
   208→  end
   209→end

    46→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    47→  rescue => e
    48→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    49→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed

    54→  # Public method for job usage
    55→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    56→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    57→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    58→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    59→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    60→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    61→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    62→      "HostConfig" => {
    63→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    64→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    65→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    66→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    67→      },
    68→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    69→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    70→      }
    71→    )
    72→
    73→    container.start
    74→    container.refresh!
    75→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    76→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    77→      container.stop rescue nil
    78→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil

    80→    end
    81→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")

    84→    begin
    85→      NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)

    93→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    94→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    95→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?

    98→    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?

### Tailscale System

Per-user Tailscale sidecars connect sandbox containers to a user's own tailnet. Each user gets their own sidecar joining **their** tailnet (not a shared one).

**Two authentication flows in `TailscaleManager`:**
- **Auth key flow** (`enable`): One-shot with `TS_AUTHKEY` env var, uses default `containerboot` entrypoint
- **Interactive login flow** (`start_login` → `check_login`): Overrides entrypoint to `tailscaled` directly, runs `tailscale up --reset --timeout=10s` to get a browser login URL, polls `tailscale status --json` for `BackendState == "Running"`

**User `tailscale_state` field:** `disabled` → `pending` (during interactive login) → `enabled`

**Sidecar architecture:**
- Bridge network: `sc-ts-net-{username}` with a random `/24` subnet drawn from `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE` (e.g. `10.89.42.0/24`)
- Container: `sc-ts-{username}` running `tailscale/tailscale:latest`
- State persisted at `/data/users/{name}/tailscale`
- Advertises subnet routes so sandboxes on the bridge are reachable from the tailnet
- `tailscale_auto_connect` user setting auto-joins new sandboxes to the bridge network

**Important:** `containerboot` (default Tailscale image entrypoint) conflicts with manual `tailscale up` when no auth key is provided. The interactive flow must override the entrypoint to run `tailscaled` directly.

### Networking Invariants — Do Not Change Without Asking

The Dockyard Docker daemon (`sc_docker`) and the Rails app share a single private address space. **All of the following must stay consistent or Tailscale sidecars (and sandbox containers) will lose internet access.**

```
SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET  (e.g. 10.89.0.0/16)
└── DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE  = same /16  (passed to dockerd --default-address-pool)
    ├── DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR  = first /24 (e.g. 10.89.0.1/24) — sc_docker0 bridge
    ├── DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR   = same /24 (e.g. 10.89.0.0/24)  — static container IPs
    ├── sandcastle-web network — allocated from the pool (e.g. 10.89.1.0/24)
    └── sc-ts-net-{user}      — random /24 from the pool (e.g. 10.89.42.0/24)
```

Perfect! Now I have gathered comprehensive information about the Tailscale sidecar networking in this Rails app. Let me provide a complete summary:

## Tailscale Sidecar Networking - Complete Analysis

I've thoroughly analyzed the Tailscale sidecar networking implementation in Sandcastle. Here's the detailed breakdown:

### 1. **TailscaleManager: Core Service** (`/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/tailscale_manager.rb`)

#### Key Attributes
- `DATA_DIR = "/data"` — persistent storage location
- `TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"` — Docker image
- `TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME` — machine name derived from `SANDCASTLE_NAME` or system hostname, slugified (e.g., `sc-staging`)

#### Bridge Network & Sidecar Creation
- **Network naming**: `sc-ts-net-{username}` (e.g., `sc-ts-net-alice`)
- **Container naming**: `sc-ts-{username}` (e.g., `sc-ts-alice`)
- **Network driver**: Docker bridge with custom IPAM config
- **Subnet**: Random `/24` drawn from `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE` (stored in DB for persistence)

content.text:
The problem is clear. The `tailscaled.state` file is owned by `nobody:nobody` (mode 600), but the current tailscaled process runs as `root`. With user namespace remapping on the dockyard daemon, root (uid 231072) can't read files owned by nobody (uid 10504).

This looks like a Tailscale image update changed which user the daemon runs as. Let me verify and fix it.

content.text:
Working now. The `10.206.10.0/24` subnet route is advertised and pings go through.

**Root cause:** The `tailscaled.state` file was owned by uid 10504 (mapped `nobody`) on the host, but the Tailscale sidecar container runs with userns base 231072 — so `root` inside the container mapped to host uid 231072, which couldn't read the 600-permission file owned by 10504. 

This likely happened because an older version of the Tailscale image ran `tailscaled` as a non-root user, then a `tailscale/tailscale:latest` update switched to running as root. Since the container uses user namespace remapping (dockyard daemon), the UID mismatch between the old nobody and new root caused the permission denied.

**Quick fix applied:** `chown 231072:231072` on the state file on the host.

Now, should we make this resilient in the code? The `restore_from_state()` or `create_sidecar()` methods could fix ownership before starting the container. Want me to add that?
Let me look at the relevant code to understand the best place to add the fix.

class TailscaleManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}

  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs → "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")
  end

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
  def restore_from_state(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state — we rely on existing saved credentials
    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) — NOT containerboot.
    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: nil
    )
    container.start

    # Re-advertise routes after tailscaled reconnects — the subnet may have changed
    # after a reinstall (Docker reallocates from the pool). No --reset so we preserve
    # credentials and other settings in the persisted state. Also re-applies the
    # canonical hostname so it reflects the current SANDCASTLE_NAME.
    container.exec([
      "sh", "-c",
      "sleep 3 && tailscale up" \
      " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
      " --accept-routes" \
      " --hostname=#{TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME}" \
      " --timeout=60s &"
    ])

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name,
      tailscale_subnet: subnet
    )
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )
    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately.
  # Called from TailscaleLoginJob — user is already in "pending" state.
  def start_login(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?

    # If there's an old container, clean up first
    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_container_id.present?

    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )

    { status: "starting" }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to start Tailscale login: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
  def check_login(user:)
    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running

    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
      subnet = subnet_for(user)
      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
      container.exec([
        "sh", "-c",
        "tailscale up --reset" \
        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
        "#{tag_flag}" \
        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
        " --timeout=120s &"
      ])
      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
    end

    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
    if status_out.first.any?
      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
      case ts_status["BackendState"]
      when "Running"
        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)
        ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
        tailscale_ip = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
        return {
          status: "authenticated",
          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
        }
      when "NeedsLogin"
        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
        if auth_url.present?
          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
        end
      end
    end

    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disable(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?

    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")

    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    cleanup_sidecar(user)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "disabled",
      tailscale_container_id: nil,
      tailscale_network: nil
    )
    delete_auth_key(user)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def status(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")

    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
    end

    result = {
      running: running,
      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
      network: user.tailscale_network,
      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
    }

    if running
      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?

      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
      if status_out.first.any?
        begin
          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
        rescue JSON::ParserError
          # Status not available yet
        end
      end
    else
      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
    end

    result
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
  end

  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    # Verify container exists and check if already connected
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return sandbox if connected_networks.key?(user.tailscale_network)

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)

    # Sysbox containers have heavier init (user namespace, rootless daemon) so
    # the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after start.
    retries = 0
    begin
      network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    rescue Docker::Error::ServerError => e
      raise unless e.message.include?("network sandbox") && retries < 3
      retries += 1
      sleep retries
      retry
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Error
    raise
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
      end
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    nil
  end

  def auth_key_path(user)
    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
  end

  private

  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
    File.write(path, auth_key)
    File.chmod(0o600, path)
  end

  def delete_auth_key(user)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    clear_tailscale_state(user)
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: auth_key,
      hostname: hostname
    )
    container.start

    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)

    [ network_name, container ]
  end

  def remove_existing_container(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
    container.delete(force: true)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # No existing container
  end

  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end

    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end
  end

  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    begin
      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    begin
      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def subnet_for(user)
    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?

    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
    begin
      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Network doesn't exist yet — fall through to generate a random /24
    end

    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
    if base
      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
    else
      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
    end
    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
  end

  def create_network(name, subnet)
    Docker::Network.get(name)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(
      name,
      "Driver" => "bridge",
      "IPAM" => {
        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
      }
    )
  end

  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME

    config = {
      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
      "name" => name,
      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
      "HostConfig" => {
        "NetworkMode" => network,
        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
        "Devices" => [
          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
        ],
        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      }
    }

    if auth_key.present?
      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
      config["Env"] = [
        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
      ]
    else
      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
      config["Cmd"] = []
    end

    Docker::Container.create(config)
  end
end

class TailscaleManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}

  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs → "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")
  end

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
  def restore_from_state(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state — we rely on existing saved credentials
    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) — NOT containerboot.
    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: nil
    )
    container.start

    # Re-advertise routes after tailscaled reconnects — the subnet may have changed
    # after a reinstall (Docker reallocates from the pool). No --reset so we preserve
    # credentials and other settings in the persisted state. Also re-applies the
    # canonical hostname so it reflects the current SANDCASTLE_NAME.
    container.exec([
      "sh", "-c",
      "sleep 3 && tailscale up" \
      " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
      " --accept-routes" \
      " --hostname=#{TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME}" \
      " --timeout=60s &"
    ])

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name,
      tailscale_subnet: subnet
    )
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )
    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately.
  # Called from TailscaleLoginJob — user is already in "pending" state.
  def start_login(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?

    # If there's an old container, clean up first
    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_container_id.present?

    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )

    { status: "starting" }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to start Tailscale login: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
  def check_login(user:)
    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running

    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
      subnet = subnet_for(user)
      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
      container.exec([
        "sh", "-c",
        "tailscale up --reset" \
        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
        "#{tag_flag}" \
        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
        " --timeout=120s &"
      ])
      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
    end

    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
    if status_out.first.any?
      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
      case ts_status["BackendState"]
      when "Running"
        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)
        ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
        tailscale_ip = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
        return {
          status: "authenticated",
          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
        }
      when "NeedsLogin"
        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
        if auth_url.present?
          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
        end
      end
    end

    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disable(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?

    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")

    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    cleanup_sidecar(user)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "disabled",
      tailscale_container_id: nil,
      tailscale_network: nil
    )
    delete_auth_key(user)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def status(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")

    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
    end

    result = {
      running: running,
      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
      network: user.tailscale_network,
      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
    }

    if running
      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?

      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
      if status_out.first.any?
        begin
          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
        rescue JSON::ParserError
          # Status not available yet
        end
      end
    else
      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
    end

    result
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
  end

  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    # Verify container exists and check if already connected
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return sandbox if connected_networks.key?(user.tailscale_network)

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)

    # Sysbox containers have heavier init (user namespace, rootless daemon) so
    # the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after start.
    retries = 0
    begin
      network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    rescue Docker::Error::ServerError => e
      raise unless e.message.include?("network sandbox") && retries < 3
      retries += 1
      sleep retries
      retry
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Error
    raise
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
      end
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    nil
  end

  def auth_key_path(user)
    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
  end

  private

  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
    File.write(path, auth_key)
    File.chmod(0o600, path)
  end

  def delete_auth_key(user)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    clear_tailscale_state(user)
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: auth_key,
      hostname: hostname
    )
    container.start

    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)

    [ network_name, container ]
  end

  def remove_existing_container(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
    container.delete(force: true)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # No existing container
  end

  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end

    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end
  end

  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    begin
      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    begin
      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def subnet_for(user)
    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?

    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
    begin
      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Network doesn't exist yet — fall through to generate a random /24
    end

    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
    if base
      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
    else
      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
    end
    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
  end

  def create_network(name, subnet)
    Docker::Network.get(name)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(
      name,
      "Driver" => "bridge",
      "IPAM" => {
        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
      }
    )
  end

  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME

    config = {
      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
      "name" => name,
      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
      "HostConfig" => {
        "NetworkMode" => network,
        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
        "Devices" => [
          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
        ],
        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      }
    }

    if auth_key.present?
      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
      config["Env"] = [
        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
      ]
    else
      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
      config["Cmd"] = []
    end

    Docker::Container.create(config)
  end
end

else
      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
      # Fix ownership first: Tailscale image updates may change which user the daemon
      # runs as (e.g. root vs nobody), leaving persisted state files unreadable.
      # With userns-remap the UID mismatch causes "permission denied" on startup.
      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "sh", "-c", "chown -R root:root /var/lib/tailscale 2>/dev/null; exec tailscaled --state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
      config["Cmd"] = []
    end

    Docker::Container.create(config)
  end
end

content.text:
The fix wraps the `tailscaled` entrypoint with `chown -R root:root /var/lib/tailscale 2>/dev/null` before exec'ing into `tailscaled`. This handles:

- **`restore_from_state`** — the main affected path where old state files have stale ownership
- **`start_login`** — harmless since `clear_tailscale_state` already removes old files, but the chown provides defense-in-depth
- The `2>/dev/null` suppresses errors if there's nothing to fix
- `exec` ensures tailscaled replaces the shell as PID 1, so signal handling (SIGTERM for graceful shutdown) still works correctly

The auth-key flow (`enable`) is unaffected since it uses the default `containerboot` entrypoint and `clear_tailscale_state` deletes any old state beforehand.

Want me to deploy this to demo?

Nothing — this is a server-side change only (`app/services/tailscale_manager.rb`). Just needs a redeploy of the Rails container. No CLI changes, no new release needed.

fix: fix Tailscale sidecar state file permission denied on image updates

Tailscale image updates can change which user tailscaled runs as (e.g.
root vs nobody). With userns-remap, persisted state files end up owned
by a different mapped UID, causing "permission denied" on startup.

Fix by chown'ing the state directory before exec'ing tailscaled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
